From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football club

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fb/Shchaslyve_Kniazha_Arena_4.jpg/250px-Shchaslyve_Kniazha_Arena_4.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shchaslyve_Kniazha_Arena_4.jpg)

Knyazha Arena in 2016 (Shakhtar - Olimpik) under-21

The **[FC Shakhtar Donetsk](/wiki/FC_Shakhtar_Donetsk "FC Shakhtar Donetsk") junior squads and academy** is a football academy and the club's junior squads of [Ukrainian](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine") professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club [Shakhtar Donetsk](/wiki/Shakhtar_Donetsk "Shakhtar Donetsk").

Shakhtar Donetsk has two junior teams (squads) that participate in the [junior championships](/wiki/Ukrainian_Premier_League_Reserves_and_Under_19 "Ukrainian Premier League Reserves and Under 19") of the [Ukrainian Premier League](/wiki/Ukrainian_Premier_League "Ukrainian Premier League"). There also were some additional reserve teams that competed in lower leagues (Shakhtar-2 and Shakhtar-3). Four more teams from the club's academy participate in the [Ukrainian Youth Football League](/wiki/Ukrainian_Youth_Football_League "Ukrainian Youth Football League") which is a separate national football league not associated with the Ukrainian Premier League designed for football academy and sports schools.

## Brief history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Shakhtar_Donetsk_junior_squads_and_academy&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Brief history")]

[FC Shakhtar Donetsk](/wiki/FC_Shakhtar_Donetsk "FC Shakhtar Donetsk") has been fielding its reserve (junior) team since 1949 when the club returned to the Soviet top league (Pervaya Gruppa). In 1953 the club established its own [training center in Kirsha](/wiki/Kirsha_Training_Centre "Kirsha Training Centre"). Since discontinuation of the [Soviet Top League](/wiki/Soviet_Top_League "Soviet Top League") in 1991, Shakhtar reserve team was discontinued as well.

In 1992 based on reserve team there was created [FC Shakhtar-2 Donetsk](/wiki/FC_Shakhtar-2_Donetsk "FC Shakhtar-2 Donetsk") which was based in [Kostiantynivka](/wiki/Kostiantynivka "Kostiantynivka") and entered competitions among professional teams the [1992 Ukrainian First League](/wiki/1992_Ukrainian_First_League "1992 Ukrainian First League").

At the end of 1990s there were more discussions about revival of junior competitions. In 1998 there was created [All-Ukrainian youth football championship](/wiki/Ukrainian_Youth_Football_League "Ukrainian Youth Football League") among senior and junior youth teams and which in 2000 Shakhtar junior youth team won. In 2001 there was established the DYuFL (Youth Football League) and teams of [Vyshcha Liha](/wiki/Ukrainian_Premier_League "Ukrainian Premier League") (Higher League) were mandated to field four teams in each age group competition (U-14, U-15, U-16, U-17). In 2002 there also was established provisional competitions among under-19 teams.

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Shakhtar_Donetsk_junior_squads_and_academy&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Honours")]

* **[UEFA Youth League](/wiki/UEFA_Youth_League "UEFA Youth League")** (Under-19)
  + Runners-up (1): [2014–15](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_UEFA_Youth_League "2014–15 UEFA Youth League")
* **[Soviet Top League](/wiki/Soviet_Top_League#Soviet_football_championship_among_reserves "Soviet Top League")** (reserves)
  + **Winners** (2): 1967, 1969
  + Runners-up (3): 1968, 1976, 1977
* **[Ukrainian Under-21 Championship](/wiki/Ukrainian_Premier_League_Reserves_and_Under_19 "Ukrainian Premier League Reserves and Under 19")**
  + **Winners** (4): 2008–09, 2010–11, 2011–12, 2017–18
  + Runners-up (5): 2006–07, 2009–10, 2013–14, 2015–16, 2016–17
* **[Ukrainian Under-19 Championship](/wiki/Ukrainian_Premier_League_Reserves_and_Under_19 "Ukrainian Premier League Reserves and Under 19")**
  + **Winners** (2): 2014–15, 2020–21
  + Runners-up (2): 2013–14, 2017–18
* **[Ukrainian Under-17 Championship](/wiki/Ukrainian_Youth_Football_League "Ukrainian Youth Football League")**
  + **Winners** (7): 2002–03, 2003–04, 2007–08, 2010–11, 2011–12, 2012–13, 2017–18
  + Runners-up (2): 2004–05, 2009–10

## International competitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Shakhtar_Donetsk_junior_squads_and_academy&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: International competitions")]

Since 2013, Shakhtar's junior team competed at the [UEFA Youth League](/wiki/UEFA_Youth_League "UEFA Youth League"). Shakhtar became the first Ukrainian team to enter the competition. The first game was played on 17 September 2013 at the [Zubieta Facilities](/wiki/Zubieta_Facilities "Zubieta Facilities") against [Real Sociedad Juvenil](/wiki/Real_Sociedad_Juvenil "Real Sociedad Juvenil"). The first goal for Shakhtar in the competition was scored by Ukrainian international footballer [Artur Miranyan](/wiki/Artur_Miranyan "Artur Miranyan"), who later switched to play for Armenia.

In 2015, Shakhtar's juniors reached the [UEFA Youth League](/wiki/UEFA_Youth_League "UEFA Youth League") finals, but lost it.
